maml:description.maml:para: Adds client secrets to machines in the Orchestrator.

The Add-OrchMachineClientSecret cmdlet adds client secrets to machines in the Orchestrator environment. Client secrets are authentication credentials that allow unattended robots to securely authenticate with the Orchestrator service.

Client secrets provide a secure authentication mechanism for unattended automation scenarios where robots need to connect to Orchestrator without user intervention. Each machine can have multiple client secrets for security rotation and backup purposes.

The cmdlet generates a new client secret for the specified machine(s) and returns the secret information that needs to be configured on the robot machines. This operation is essential for setting up secure unattended robot authentication.
Primary Endpoint: POST /api/clientsecrets/{licenseKey}
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - 必要なOAuthスコープ]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - 必要な権限]

Specifies the names of the machines to which client secrets will be added. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, client secrets will be added to all matching machines.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is important when adding client secrets as it affects machine authentication configurations.

Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to add client secrets to machines in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which machines would have client secrets added before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is important when adding client secrets as it affects machine authentication configurations.

Specifies the names of the machines to which client secrets will be added. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, client secrets will be added to all matching machines.

Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to add client secrets to machines in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which machines would have client secrets added before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects machines across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.

The generated client secrets must be securely stored and configured on the corresponding robot machines. The secret information is sensitive and should be handled according to your organization's security policies.
Client secrets enable unattended robot authentication. Ensure that only authorized personnel have access to the generated secret information.
For security reasons, consider implementing regular client secret rotation using this cmdlet combined with Remove-OrchMachineClientSecret.
Use the -WhatIf parameter to preview the operation, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple machines.
The returned MachineSecretKey object contains all necessary information for configuring robot authentication. Store this information securely immediately after generation.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a new client secret to the machine named "ProductionMachine".
Adds new client secrets to multiple machines specified in a comma-separated list.
Shows what would happen if client secrets were added to all machines whose names start with "Prod", without actually performing the operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Add-OrchMachineClientSecret TestMachine -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a client secret to the machine named "TestMachine" in multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> $secret = Add-OrchMachineClientSecret AutomationServer -Confirm PS C:\> $secret | Select-Object MachineKey, ClientSecret

dev:remarks.maml:para: Adds a client secret with confirmation prompt and captures the returned secret information for configuration.
Identifies standard machines and shows what client secrets would be added without actually performing the operation.

maml:description.maml:para: Clear the in-memory cache in UiPathOrch drives.

The UiPathOrch module caches entities retrieved from the Orchestrator on each drive to optimize response times and reduce the load on the Orchestrator server. In particular, this cache is used to display appropriate candidates for auto-completion of parameter values.

If you want the PowerShell console to reflect updates to entities on Orchestrator (such as creating or removing folders) made via Orchestrator Web or other external applications, clear the in-memory cache with this cmdlet.

For the -Path parameter, specify the drive on which you want to clear the cache. If the -Path parameter is not specified, the cache on the current drive is cleared. If the current drive is not an UiPathOrch drive, the cache on all UiPathOrch drives are cleared.
Primary Endpoint: (none)
OAuth required scopes: (none)
Required permissions: (none)
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Clears the in-memory cache on the Orch1: as the current drive.
Clears the in-memory cache on all drives managed by UiPathOrch, because the current drive (C:) is not an UiPathOrch drive.
Clears the in-memory cache on the Orch1: and Orch2: drives.
The current drive can be specified using a period (.), which represents the current location. In this command, the cache is cleared on both the current drive (Orch1:) and Orch2:.

maml:description.maml:para: Copies machine templates between UiPath Orchestrator tenants.

The `Copy-OrchMachine` cmdlet copies machine templates from the current UiPath Orchestrator tenant to one or more destination tenants. This is useful for replicating machine configurations across multiple Orchestrator environments or when setting up new tenants with standardized machine templates.

The cmdlet operates on tenant entities, copying machine templates including their configuration settings such as slots allocation, scope, automation type, and other properties. Note that sensitive information like license keys and client secrets are regenerated for the destination tenant.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Machines
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Write
Required permissions: Machines.Create
Specifies the name(s) of the machine templates to copy. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can specify multiple machine names using comma-separated values.
Specifies the destination tenant(s) where the machine templates will be copied. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch2:, Orch3:). You can specify multiple destinations using comma-separated values.
Specifies the source tenant path. If not specified, the current tenant is used. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:).
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by the cmdlet.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.
Specifies the destination tenant(s) where the machine templates will be copied. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch2:, Orch3:). You can specify multiple destinations using comma-separated values.
Specifies the name(s) of the machine templates to copy. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can specify multiple machine names using comma-separated values.

maml:alert.maml:para: Important Security Considerations: - License keys and client secrets are regenerated in the destination tenant for security reasons
- The copied machine templates will have new unique identifiers
- Ensure you have appropriate permissions in both source and destination tenants Machine Template Properties Copied: - Name and Description
- Machine Type and Scope
- Slot allocations (Unattended, NonProduction, TestAutomation, etc.)
- Automation Type and Target Framework settings
- Update Policy settings
- Tags and maintenance window configurations Use Cases: - Standardizing machine configurations across multiple tenants
- Setting up new Orchestrator environments with existing templates
- Migrating machine templates between development and production environments
- Creating backup copies of critical machine configurations Best Practices: - Use -WhatIf to preview changes before execution
- Use -Confirm for critical production operations
- Verify machine template functionality in destination tenant after copying
- Update any tenant-specific configurations after copying
Use Get-OrchMachine to view available machine templates before copying.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Copy-OrchMachine "[Default] Agent Service" Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Copies the "[Default] Agent Service" machine template to the Orch2 tenant.
Shows what would happen when copying all machine templates with "Template" in their name to both Orch2 and Orch3 tenants, without actually performing the copy operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachine | Where-Object {$_.Type -eq "Template"} | Copy-OrchMachine Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine templates and copies them to Orch2 tenant using pipeline input.
Copies the "Production Template" machine to Orch2 with confirmation prompt before execution.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Copy-OrchMachine -Path Orch1: "Cloud Robot Template" Orch2:, Orch3:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Copies the "Cloud Robot Template" from Orch1 tenant to both Orch2 and Orch3 tenants, specifying the source tenant explicitly.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables inheritance for a FolderMachine, preventing it from propagating to subfolders.

Disables inheritance for a specified FolderMachine, setting the PropagateToSubFolders property to false. This prevents the machine configuration from being inherited by subfolders within the folder hierarchy.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.ToggleFolderMachineInherit
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Write
Required permissions: (Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit - Propagate machine to subfolders only if Units.Edit permission is provided or only if SubFolders.Edit permission on all folders provided)
This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either navigation to the target folder or specification of target folders using -Path parameter.

Specifies the name of the FolderMachine to disable inheritance for. This parameter accepts wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Since this is a positional parameter, the parameter name can be omitted.
`yaml Type: String[] Parameter Sets: (All) Aliases:
Required: True Position: 0 Default value: None Accept pipeline input: True (ByPropertyName) Accept wildcard characters: True
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
`yaml Type: SwitchParameter Parameter Sets: (All) Aliases: cf
Required: False Position: Named Default value: None Accept pipeline input: False Accept wildcard characters: False

Specifies the folder paths where to search for FolderMachines. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.

Required: False Position: Named Default value: None Accept pipeline input: True (ByPropertyName) Accept wildcard characters: True

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either: - Navigation to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), OR - Specification of target folders using the -Path parameter Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path before the Name parameter when using both parameters.

The PropagateToSubFolders property determines whether a FolderMachine configuration is inherited by subfolders. When disabled, subfolders within the folder hierarchy will not inherit this machine configuration.
Use Get-OrchFolderMachine to verify the current inheritance status by checking the PropagateToSubFolders property.
For safety, consider using -WhatIf to preview the changes before execution.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Disables inheritance for the machine named "ProductionMachine" in the current folder, preventing it from propagating to subfolders.
### Example 2 `powershell PS C:\> Disable-OrchFolderMachineInherit -Path Orch1:\Development TestMachine

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables inheritance for the machine named "TestMachine" in the Development folder without changing the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Shows what would happen when disabling inheritance for multiple machines without actually executing the command.
### Example 4 `powershell PS Orch1:\Production> Get-OrchFolderMachine ProductionMachine | ConvertTo-Json | Select-String PropagateToSubFolders

dev:remarks.maml:para: Verifies the current inheritance status by checking the PropagateToSubFolders property after running the cmdlet.

maml:description.maml:para: Disables webhooks in the Orchestrator.

The Disable-OrchWebhook cmdlet disables previously enabled webhooks in the Orchestrator environment. When a webhook is disabled, it will stop sending HTTP notifications to external systems, effectively pausing the integration without removing the webhook configuration.

Disabling webhooks is useful for temporarily stopping notifications during maintenance periods, troubleshooting integration issues, or when external systems are unavailable. The webhook configuration remains intact and can be re-enabled later using the Enable-OrchWebhook cmdlet.
The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before disabling webhooks.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Webhooks({webhookId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks
Required permissions: Webhooks.Edit
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be disabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be disabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when disabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to disable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be disabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when disabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be disabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be disabled.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to disable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be disabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects webhooks across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
Once disabled, webhooks will immediately stop sending notifications. Any events that occur while the webhook is disabled will not be sent to the target URL.
Use Get-OrchWebhook to verify the current status of webhooks before and after disabling them.
Consider using -WhatIf to preview the operation, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple webhooks.
Disabling webhooks is a reversible operation. Use Enable-OrchWebhook to re-enable webhooks when needed.
Temporary disabling of webhooks is preferable to removal when you plan to use the same webhook configuration again in the future.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables the webhook named "IntegrationHook".
Disables multiple webhooks by specifying their names in a comma-separated list.
Shows what would happen if all webhooks whose names start with "Test" were disabled, without actually performing the operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Disable-OrchWebhook MaintenanceHook -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables the webhook named "MaintenanceHook" in multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchWebhook | Where-Object Enabled -eq $true | Disable-OrchWebhook -Confirm

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds all enabled webhooks and disables them with confirmation prompts for each operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Disable-OrchWebhook CriticalEventsHook | Select-Object Name, Enabled, Url

dev:remarks.maml:para: Disables a webhook and displays its updated status including name, enabled state, and URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Opens the UiPath Orchestrator configuration file for editing.

The Edit-OrchConfig cmdlet opens the UiPathOrchConfig.json configuration file in the default or specified editor. This file contains the connection configurations for all UiPath Orchestrator drives, including URLs, authentication settings, and other connection parameters.
The configuration file is located at: ([Environment]::GetFolderPath('MyDocuments'))\PowerShell\Modules\UiPathOrch\UiPathOrchConfig.json
Primary Endpoint: N/A (Local file operation)
OAuth required scopes: N/A
Required permissions: File system write access to user documents folder
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet.
Specifies the editor to use for opening the configuration file. Valid values are "Notepad" (uses Windows Notepad) and "Default" (uses the default editor associated with .json files).
Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by this cmdlet.
Specifies the editor to use for opening the configuration file. Valid values are "Notepad" (uses Windows Notepad) and "Default" (uses the default editor associated with .json files).

maml:alert.maml:para: After modifying the configuration file: 1. Save the file in the editor 2. Restart PowerShell session 3. Import required modules: Import-Module UiPathOrch, PSReadLine 4. Verify configuration with Get-OrchPSDrive 5. Test connection with Get-OrchCurrentUser
The configuration file contains sensitive information including OAuth settings. Ensure proper file permissions and avoid sharing this file.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 1: Open configuration file in default editor -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the UiPathOrchConfig.json file in the default text editor (typically Notepad on Windows).

command:example.maml:title: -------------- Example 2: Initial setup workflow --------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> # Check if drives are already configured PS C:\> Get-OrchPSDrive PS C:\> # If no drives found, edit configuration PS C:\> if ((Get-OrchPSDrive).Count -eq 0) { Write-Host "No drives configured. Opening configuration editor..." Edit-OrchConfig Write-Host "After saving changes, restart PowerShell and import modules:" Write-Host "Import-Module UiPathOrch, PSReadLine" }

dev:remarks.maml:para: Shows the typical initial setup workflow when no drives are configured.
Opens the configuration file using Windows Notepad.

command:example.maml:title: ----------- Example 4: Open with default JSON editor -----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the configuration file using the default editor associated with .json files (e.g., Visual Studio Code, Notepad++, or other JSON editors).

maml:description.maml:para: Opens process details for editing in the default web browser.

The `Edit-OrchProcess` cmdlet opens the process details page in the default web browser for editing the specified process(es). This provides a convenient way to navigate from PowerShell to the Orchestrator web interface to modify process settings, view process details, or access editing features not available through the command line.
This cmdlet is useful for quickly navigating from PowerShell to the web interface to edit process configurations, update process settings, or access the visual process designer.
Multiple process names can be specified to open multiple process editing pages simultaneously. You can use autocomplete for process names by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - Browser navigation, no direct API endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Not applicable for browser navigation]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Process edit permissions for browser access]
Specifies the name(s) of the processes to be edited in the browser. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the target folder path(s). If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name(s) of the processes to be edited in the browser. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the target folder path(s). If not specified, the current folder will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This is a utility cmdlet for navigating from PowerShell to the Orchestrator web interface
- The cmdlet requires an active web browser and internet connectivity to the Orchestrator instance
- Multiple process names will open multiple browser tabs or windows depending on browser settings
- The user must have appropriate permissions to edit processes in the web interface
- Wildcard patterns are supported for process names

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\MyWorkspace PS Orch1:\MyWorkspace> Edit-OrchProcess -Name "MsgBox"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Opens the process editing page for the "MsgBox" process in the default web browser.
Opens process editing pages for multiple processes in separate browser tabs or windows.
Gets all processes with "Agent" in their name and opens their editing pages.
Opens editing pages for all processes whose names start with "My" using wildcard pattern matching.
Opens the "MsgBox" process editing page from multiple specified folders.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables inheritance for a FolderMachine, allowing it to propagate to subfolders.
Enables inheritance for a specified FolderMachine, setting the PropagateToSubFolders property to true. This allows the machine configuration to be inherited by subfolders within the folder hierarchy.
Primary Endpoint: POST /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.ToggleFolderMachineInherit
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Write
Required permissions: Units.Edit or SubFolders.Edit
This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either navigation to the target folder or specification of target folders using -Path parameter.

Specifies the name of the FolderMachine to enable inheritance for. This parameter accepts wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Since this is a positional parameter, the parameter name can be omitted.
`yaml Type: String[] Parameter Sets: (All) Aliases:
Required: True Position: 0 Default value: None Accept pipeline input: True (ByPropertyName) Accept wildcard characters: True
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.
`yaml Type: SwitchParameter Parameter Sets: (All) Aliases: cf
Required: False Position: Named Default value: None Accept pipeline input: False Accept wildcard characters: False

Specifies the folder paths where to search for FolderMachines. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
`yaml Type: String[] Parameter Sets: (All) Aliases:
Required: False Position: Named Default value: None Accept pipeline input: True (ByPropertyName) Accept wildcard characters: True
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run.

Required: False Position: Named Default value: None Accept pipeline input: False Accept wildcard characters: False

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either: - Navigation to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), OR - Specification of target folders using the -Path parameter Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path before the Name parameter when using both parameters.

The PropagateToSubFolders property determines whether a FolderMachine configuration is inherited by subfolders. When enabled, subfolders within the folder hierarchy will inherit this machine configuration.
Use Get-OrchFolderMachine to verify the current inheritance status by checking the PropagateToSubFolders property.
For safety, consider using -WhatIf to preview the changes before execution.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Enables inheritance for the machine named "ProductionMachine" in the current folder, allowing it to propagate to subfolders.
### Example 2 `powershell PS C:\> Enable-OrchFolderMachineInherit -Path Orch1:\Production ProductionMachine

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables inheritance for the machine named "ProductionMachine" in the Production folder without changing the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code:
Shows what would happen when enabling inheritance for multiple machines without actually executing the command.
### Example 4 `powershell PS Orch1:\Production> Get-OrchFolderMachine ProductionMachine | ConvertTo-Json | Select-String PropagateToSubFolders

dev:remarks.maml:para: Verifies the current inheritance status by checking the PropagateToSubFolders property after running the cmdlet.

maml:description.maml:para: Enables the webhooks.

The Enable-OrchWebhook cmdlet enables previously disabled webhooks in the Orchestrator environment. Webhooks allow Orchestrator to send HTTP notifications to external systems when specific events occur, enabling real-time integration with external applications and services.

When a webhook is enabled, it will start sending notifications for the events it is configured to monitor. This includes events such as job completion, process deployment, robot status changes, and other Orchestrator activities based on the webhook's configuration.
The cmdlet supports safety features like -WhatIf to preview the operation and -Confirm to request confirmation before enabling webhooks.
Primary Endpoint: PATCH /odata/Webhooks({webhookId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Webhooks
Required permissions: Webhooks.Edit
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be enabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be enabled.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when enabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to enable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be enabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet. This is useful when enabling multiple webhooks or when you want to ensure the operation is intentional.
Specifies the names of the webhooks to be enabled. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching webhooks will be enabled.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to enable webhooks in multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet is not run. Use this parameter to preview which webhooks would be enabled before actually performing the operation.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it affects webhooks across the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.

Once enabled, webhooks will immediately start sending notifications based on their configured event types and filters. Ensure that the target URLs are accessible and ready to receive webhook notifications before enabling them.
Use Get-OrchWebhook to verify the current status of webhooks before and after enabling them.
Consider using -WhatIf to preview the operation, especially when using wildcard patterns that might match multiple webhooks.
If a webhook's target URL is no longer valid or accessible, enabling it may result in failed notification attempts and potential performance impacts.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the webhook named "JobCompletionHook".
Enables multiple webhooks by specifying their names in a comma-separated list.
Shows what would happen if all webhooks whose names start with "Test" were enabled, without actually performing the operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Enable-OrchWebhook ProductionHook -Path Orch1:, Orch2:

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables the webhook named "ProductionHook" in multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchWebhook | Where-Object Enabled -eq $false | Enable-OrchWebhook -Confirm

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds all disabled webhooks and enables them with confirmation prompts for each operation.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Enable-OrchWebhook CriticalEventsHook | Select-Object Name, Enabled, Url

dev:remarks.maml:para: Enables a webhook and displays its updated status including name, enabled state, and URL.

maml:description.maml:para: Finds folders that have no direct user assignments in UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned` cmdlet searches for folders within UiPath Orchestrator that have no users directly assigned to them. This is useful for identifying orphaned folders, conducting security audits, and ensuring proper folder access management across the organization.

The cmdlet analyzes folder structures and user assignments to identify folders that may be inaccessible to users or require attention from administrators. By default, it only considers direct user assignments, but can optionally include inherited permissions from parent folders.

This cmdlet operates on tenant entities and can be run from any location within an Orchestrator drive. It provides comprehensive folder information including folder type, feed type, and provision type to help administrators understand the nature of unassigned folders.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders with custom filtering logic
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: Folders.View
Specifies the tenant path to search. If not specified, the current tenant is used. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:).
When specified, includes folders that might inherit user permissions from parent folders in the analysis. By default, only direct user assignments are considered.
Specifies the tenant path to search. If not specified, the current tenant is used. Use tenant drive names (e.g., Orch1:, Orch2:).
When specified, includes folders that might inherit user permissions from parent folders in the analysis. By default, only direct user assignments are considered.

maml:alert.maml:para: Important Administrative Use Cases: - Security Audits : Identify folders that may be inaccessible to users - Cleanup Operations : Find orphaned folders that can be safely removed - Access Management : Ensure all folders have appropriate user assignments - Compliance : Verify that organizational access policies are properly implemented Folder Information Returned: - Path: Full path to the folder within the tenant
- DisplayName: Human-readable name of the folder
- Id: Unique identifier for the folder
- FolderType: Type of folder (Standard, Personal, Solution, etc.)
- FeedType: Feed type (Processes, FolderHierarchy, PersonalWorkspace, etc.)
- ProvisionType: How the folder was created (Manual, Automatic)
- PermissionModel: Permission model applied to the folder Common Folder Types: - Standard: Regular organizational folders
- Personal: User-specific personal workspace folders
- Solution: Folders associated with UiPath Solutions
- Department: Departmental folders Common Feed Types: - Processes: Folders designed for storing automation processes
- FolderHierarchy: Structural folders for organization
- PersonalWorkspace: Individual user workspace folders Interpretation Guidelines: - Personal workspace folders with no assignments may be normal (user-specific)
- Standard process folders with no assignments typically require attention
- Solution folders may have specialized permission models
- Automatically provisioned folders might be system-generated Best Practices: - Run this cmdlet periodically as part of access management reviews
- Investigate Standard folders with no assignments for potential security issues
- Consider using -IncludeInherited to understand the complete permission picture

- Document any intentionally unassigned folders for future reference Important Note about Path Selection: When using Select-Object with the results, always include Path as the first property to identify which tenant and location each folder belongs to. This is essential for managing folders across multiple tenants.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete folder object structure including detailed permission and configuration information.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds all folders in the current tenant that have no direct user assignments.
Finds folders with no user assignments, including those that might inherit permissions from parent folders.
Finds folders with no user assignments in the Orch1 tenant, specifying the path explicitly.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned | Where-Object {$_.FolderType -eq "Standard"} | Select-Object Path, DisplayName, FolderType, ProvisionType

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds unassigned folders, filters for Standard folders only, and displays key properties. Note that Path is selected first to identify the location of each folder.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned | Group-Object FolderType | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups unassigned folders by type and shows the count for each type.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned | Where-Object {$_.ProvisionType -eq "Manual"} | Format-Table DisplayName, FolderType, Description

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds manually provisioned folders with no user assignments and displays them in a formatted table.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $unassignedFolders = Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned PS Orch1:\> $unassignedFolders | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all unassigned folders and displays the complete object structure for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Find-OrchFolderNoUserAssigned | Where-Object {$_.FeedType -eq "Processes"} | Select-Object Path, DisplayName, Description, Id

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds process-type folders with no user assignments and displays their key identification information.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets extractors of Document Understanding from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-DuExtractor` cmdlet retrieves extractor information from UiPath Document Understanding projects in Orchestrator. Extractors are machine learning models or rule-based components that extract specific data fields from documents during the document processing workflow.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities within Document Understanding projects and requires either navigation to the target project folder or specification of target folders using -Path, -Recurse parameters. It can retrieve both custom extractors created within the project and pretrained extractors available for use.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/ML/Extractors
OAuth required scopes: OR.ML or OR.ML.Read
Required permissions: ML.Extractor.View
Specifies the name(s) of the extractors to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the Document Understanding project path(s) to search for extractors. Supports wildcards and multiple values. If not specified, the current location is used.
Includes extractors from all subprojects when retrieving extractors. This is a folder entity operation parameter.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the name(s) of the extractors to retrieve. Supports wildcards (* and ?) for pattern matching. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the Document Understanding project path(s) to search for extractors. Supports wildcards and multiple values. If not specified, the current location is used.
Includes extractors from all subprojects when retrieving extractors. This is a folder entity operation parameter.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities within Document Understanding projects and requires either: - Navigation to the target project using Set-Location (cd), OR - Specification of target projects using the -Path or -Recurse parameters Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path or -Recurse before other parameters when using multiple parameters. Extractor Information: - Name: The display name of the extractor
- ExtractorType: Type of extractor (MachineLearning, Regex, etc.)
- Status: Current status (Published, Draft, Training, etc.)
- CreatedBy: User who created the extractor
- CreatedDate: When the extractor was created
- Version: Current version of the extractor Common Extractor Types: - MachineLearning: AI-based extractors trained on labeled data
- Regex: Rule-based extractors using regular expressions
- FormExtractor: Extractors for structured forms
- KeywordBased: Extractors that look for specific keywords Common Status Values: - Published: Extractor is ready for use in production
- Draft: Extractor is in development
- Training: Machine learning extractor is being trained
- Failed: Training or deployment failed Use Cases: - Monitor extractor development across projects
- Identify available extractors for document processing workflows
- Check training status of machine learning extractors

- Audit extractor usage and performance across the organization Important Note about Path Selection: When using Select-Object with folder entities, always include Path as the first property to identify which project each extractor belongs to. This is essential for managing extractors across multiple Document Understanding projects.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete extractor object structure including detailed configuration and performance metrics.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all extractors in the current Document Understanding project.
Gets all extractors from all Document Understanding projects recursively.
Gets extractors whose names start with "Invoice" using wildcard pattern matching.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1Du:\> Get-DuExtractor -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.ExtractorType -eq "MachineLearning"} | Select-Object Path, Name, ExtractorType, Status

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine learning extractors from all projects and displays key properties. Note that Path is selected first to identify which project each extractor belongs to.
Gets only published extractors from the current project.
Gets extractors from specific Document Understanding projects without changing the current location.
Gets all extractors and displays the complete object structure including detailed properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1Du:\> Get-DuExtractor -Recurse | Group-Object ExtractorType | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups extractors by type and shows the count for each type across all projects.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Document Understanding roles from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-DuRole` cmdlet retrieves Document Understanding role information from UiPath Orchestrator. Document Understanding roles define the permissions and access levels for users working with document processing projects, classifiers, extractors, and document types.

This cmdlet returns comprehensive role information including role descriptions, creation details, permission types (BUILTIN vs custom), and detailed action permissions. Each role contains specific actionDetails that define granular permissions for various Document Understanding operations such as reading projects, managing classifiers, updating document types, and monitoring project performance.
The cmdlet operates on Document Understanding drives (Orch1Du:) and supports filtering by role name using wildcards.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /{partitionGlobalId}/pap_/api/roles?scopeType=project&serviceName=DocumentUnderstanding
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Document Understanding role scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Document Understanding role permissions]

Specifies the name(s) of the Document Understanding roles to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all roles are returned.

Specifies the name of the target Document Understanding drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Document Understanding instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

Specifies the name(s) of the Document Understanding roles to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all roles are returned.

Specifies the name of the target Document Understanding drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Document Understanding instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet operates on Document Understanding drives (Orch1Du:)
- Common built-in roles include: DU Data Annotator, DU Developer, DU Model Trainer, DU Project Administrator, and DU Viewer
- The actionDetails property contains granular permissions for specific Document Understanding operations
- Role types include BUILTIN (predefined system roles) and custom roles created by administrators
- Each action detail specifies namespace (DOCUMENTUNDERSTANDING), resource actions (Read, Update, Create, Delete), and resource groups
- Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete actionDetails structure and understand specific permissions

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1Du:\ PS Orch1Du:\> Get-DuRole

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all Document Understanding roles available in the current instance.
Gets Document Understanding roles whose names contain "Developer".

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1Du:\> $role = Get-DuRole -Name "DU Data Annotator" PS Orch1Du:\> $role.actionDetails | Select-Object name, description | Format-Table

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the DU Data Annotator role and displays its specific action permissions in a formatted table.
Gets only built-in Document Understanding roles (as opposed to custom roles).

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1Du:\> $role = Get-DuRole | Select-Object -First 1 PS Orch1Du:\> $role | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the first role and displays its complete structure in JSON format to explore all properties including detailed actionDetails.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets Document Understanding users from UiPath Orchestrator projects.

The `Get-DuUser` cmdlet retrieves Document Understanding user information from UiPath Orchestrator Document Understanding projects. This cmdlet shows users and groups that have been assigned roles within specific Document Understanding projects, including their role assignments, inheritance information, and security principal details.

This is a folder entity operation cmdlet that requires either navigating to a specific Document Understanding project folder or using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters to specify target projects. The cmdlet operates within the context of Document Understanding projects and returns users with their assigned roles and permissions within those projects.

The cmdlet returns comprehensive user information including role assignments (roleAssignmentDtos), inheritance status, security principal IDs, and project-specific permissions. Each user may have multiple role assignments with different scopes and inheritance properties.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - Document Understanding users API endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Document Understanding user scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Document Understanding user permissions]

Specifies the name(s) of the Document Understanding users to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all users are returned.

Specifies the Document Understanding project path(s). If not specified, the current location is used. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple project paths.
Includes users from subprojects when retrieving users. This is a folder entity operation parameter that allows scanning multiple projects.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
{{ Fill CsvEncoding Description }}
{{ Fill ExportCsv Description }}

Specifies the name(s) of the Document Understanding users to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all users are returned.

Specifies the Document Understanding project path(s). If not specified, the current location is used. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple project paths.
Includes users from subprojects when retrieving users. This is a folder entity operation parameter that allows scanning multiple projects.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This is a folder entity operation cmdlet that requires navigation to a Document Understanding project or use of -Path/-Recurse/-Depth parameters
- If you see the error "Use Set-Location cmdlet to navigate to the target folder first...", you need to either navigate to a project folder or use the folder operation parameters
- User types include DirectoryUser (individual users) and DirectoryGroup (groups)
- The roleAssignmentDtos property contains detailed role assignment information including inheritance status and scope
- Role assignments can be inherited from organizational units or directly assigned to the project
- Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete roleAssignmentDtos structure and understand role details
- The securityPrincipalId links users to their security context within the Document Understanding system

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location "Orch1Du:\MyProject" PS Orch1Du:\MyProject> Get-DuUser

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all users assigned to the current Document Understanding project.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1Du:\ PS Orch1Du:\> Get-DuUser -Recurse

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all users from all Document Understanding projects recursively.
Gets users from a specific Document Understanding project without changing the current location.
Gets only individual users (not groups) from the current Document Understanding project.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1Du:\MyProject> $user = Get-DuUser | Where-Object displayName -eq "Yoko Tsuda" PS Orch1Du:\MyProject> $user | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets a specific user and displays their complete structure in JSON format to explore role assignments and security details.
Gets all users across projects who have direct (non-inherited) role assignments.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets action catalogs from the Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchActionCatalog cmdlet retrieves action catalogs from the Orchestrator environment. Action catalogs are repositories that store and organize automation actions, workflows, and task definitions for process mining and task mining scenarios.

Action catalogs provide structured storage for reusable automation components and enable centralized management of action libraries. They support features like encryption, retention policies, and hierarchical organization through folders.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive traversal to find action catalogs across different folder hierarchies. Each action catalog includes metadata such as retention policies, encryption status, and folder organization.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/TaskCatalogs
OAuth required scopes: OR.Tasks or OR.Tasks.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - 必要な権限]

Specifies the names of the action catalogs to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching action catalogs will be returned.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for action catalogs. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Recursively gets action catalogs from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the names of the action catalogs to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching action catalogs will be returned.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for action catalogs. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Recursively gets action catalogs from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
Action catalogs support important features including: - Encryption for sensitive automation components
- Retention policies with configurable actions (Delete, Archive)
- Hierarchical organization through folder structures
- Centralized management of reusable automation actions
Parameter placement is important for folder entity cmdlets. Place -Path, -Recurse, -Depth parameters immediately after the cmdlet name for proper auto-completion functionality.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of action catalog objects, as they contain detailed metadata including retention settings and organizational information.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all action catalogs from the current folder.
Retrieves the action catalog named "MyActionCatalog" from the current folder.
Retrieves all action catalogs whose names start with "Process" using wildcard matching.
Retrieves all action catalogs from the current folder and all subfolders recursively.
Retrieves all action catalogs from the specified workspace and its subfolders.
Retrieves action catalogs recursively but limits the search to 2 levels deep.
Finds all encrypted action catalogs across all accessible folders.
Retrieves all action catalogs and displays their complete structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 9 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchActionCatalog -Recurse | Select-Object Path, Name, FoldersCount, RetentionAction, RetentionPeriod | Format-Table

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays action catalogs with their key properties in a formatted table.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the activity settings from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchActivitySetting` cmdlet retrieves the activity-related configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes the API version used for activities, SignalR connection settings for real-time communication with activities, and related configuration options.
These settings control how activities communicate with Orchestrator and manage real-time signaling for activity execution and monitoring.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetActivitySettings
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Activity settings view permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - The ApiVersion property indicates the API version used for activity communication
- The SignalR.Url property contains the URL for real-time SignalR communication
- The SignalR.SkipNegotiation property controls whether SignalR negotiation is bypassed
- These settings are essential for proper activity execution and real-time monitoring

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchActivitySetting

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the activity settings for the current Orchestrator instance.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $activitySetting = Get-OrchActivitySetting PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "API Version: $($activitySetting.ApiVersion)"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the activity settings and displays the API version.
Gets the activity settings and displays all the key configuration properties.
Gets the activity settings and displays them in a detailed list format.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the API triggers.

Outputs information about API triggers with specified names in the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no API trigger names are specified, it outputs all API triggers in the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the assets from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchAsset` cmdlet retrieves asset information from UiPath Orchestrator. Assets are data stores that can be used by robots to store configuration settings, credentials, and other data needed for automation processes.

This cmdlet supports filtering by asset name and value type, expanding user-specific values, and exporting results to CSV files. It can operate on specific folders or recursively across folder hierarchies.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Assets
OAuth required scopes: OR.Assets or OR.Assets.Read
Required permissions: Assets.View
Specifies the name(s) of the assets to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the encoding for CSV export operations. Common values include UTF8, UTF8BOM, ASCII.

Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. For example, 1 covers the current folder and immediate subfolders.
Exports credential asset data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be imported later using Set-OrchCredentialAsset.
Exports the asset data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be imported later using Set-OrchAsset.
Specifies the folder path(s) to search for assets. Supports wildcards and multiple values. If not specified, the current location is used.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Includes assets from subfolders when retrieving assets. This is a folder entity operation parameter.
Specifies the value type(s) of assets to retrieve. Valid values include: Text, Bool, Integer, Credential.
Expands and displays user-specific values for per-robot assets. This shows the actual values assigned to each user/machine combination for assets with PerRobot scope.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export operations. Common values include UTF8, UTF8BOM, ASCII.

Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included. For example, 1 covers the current folder and immediate subfolders.
Exports credential asset data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be imported later using Set-OrchCredentialAsset.
Exports the asset data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be imported later using Set-OrchAsset.
Specifies the name(s) of the assets to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the folder path(s) to search for assets. Supports wildcards and multiple values. If not specified, the current location is used.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Includes assets from subfolders when retrieving assets. This is a folder entity operation parameter.
Specifies the value type(s) of assets to retrieve. Valid values include: Text, Bool, Integer, Credential.
Expands and displays user-specific values for per-robot assets. This shows the actual values assigned to each user/machine combination for assets with PerRobot scope.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Assets can have different value types: Text, Bool, Integer, and Credential
- The ValueScope property indicates whether the asset is Global or PerRobot
- PerRobot assets can have different values for each user/machine combination
- Use -ExpandUserValues to see the actual values for PerRobot assets
- Some asset fields are nested and may require expansion to see complete information
- The -ExportCsv and -ExportCredentialCsv parameters create files that can be used with Set-OrchAsset and Set-OrchCredentialAsset for bulk operations

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\ PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchAsset -Recurse

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all assets from all folders in the current Orchestrator instance recursively.
Gets all assets recursively and expands user-specific values for per-robot assets.

Gets assets whose names start with "my" and end with "asset" using wildcard pattern matching. To display an asset with an asterisk in its name literally, escape it with a backtick: `Get-OrchAsset "my`*asset"`.
Gets assets whose names start with "a" or "y" from all folders recursively.
Gets all assets recursively and displays them in a detailed list format.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all assets recursively and displays the Path, Name, and Value properties. Note that Path is selected first to identify which folder each asset belongs to.
Gets all assets recursively and converts the output to JSON format.
Filters to show only assets that have a boolean value of False.
Gets all assets recursively and redirects the output to a text file.
Gets all assets recursively and exports them to a CSV file.
Gets all assets recursively and exports credential assets to a separate CSV file.
Gets only Text and Boolean type assets from all folders recursively.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets authentication settings.

The `Get-OrchAuthenticationSetting` cmdlet retrieves authentication configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. These settings control various aspects of user authentication, password policies, external authentication integration, and access token management.

The cmdlet returns authentication-related settings including password change policies, self-service capabilities, external logout configuration, and token authentication enablement for different types of access (user, robot, and service-to-service authentication). Additionally, it includes basic application settings, build information, localization settings, and telemetry configuration.

Key authentication settings include controls for password management (Auth.AllowChangePassword), user self-service features (Auth.AllowSelfEmailUpdate), external authentication integration (Auth.ExternalLogoutUrl), and access token authentication enablement for various client types (UserAccessTokenAuthentication.Enabled, RobotAccessTokenAuthentication.Enabled, S2SAccessTokenAuthentication.Enabled).

These settings are essential for understanding the authentication landscape of the Orchestrator instance, configuring security policies, troubleshooting authentication issues, and ensuring proper integration with external identity providers and authentication systems.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the credential stores.

The `Get-OrchCredentialStore` cmdlet retrieves credential store information from UiPath Orchestrator. Credential stores are secure repositories used to store and manage credentials and sensitive information that can be accessed by robots and automation processes.

The cmdlet returns comprehensive information about configured credential stores including their types (Database, AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, etc.), configuration details, read-only status, and proxy settings when applicable. Credential stores provide centralized, secure credential management for automation workflows.

Orchestrator supports multiple types of credential stores including the built-in Orchestrator Database store and external secure storage services like AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, and HashiCorp Vault. Each store type has specific configuration requirements and capabilities.

The cmdlet provides essential information for understanding the credential management landscape, configuring robot access to credentials, and ensuring proper security configuration across automation environments.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/CredentialStores, /odata/CredentialStores({credentialStoreId})
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Credential store scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Credential store view permissions]
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/CredentialStores, /odata/CredentialStores({credentialStoreId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: Settings.View
Specifies the Name of the credential stores to be retrieved.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.
{{ Fill ProgressAction Description }}
Specifies the Name of the credential stores to be retrieved.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the user connected to the tenant of the target drive.

The `Get-OrchCurrentUser` cmdlet retrieves information about the currently authenticated user for the specified Orchestrator drive. This includes user details such as ID, username, full name, email address, user type, and assigned roles.

This cmdlet can only be used for drives connected with non-confidential application settings (user-scoped authentication). It cannot be used with confidential application drives (app-scoped authentication).
The current user may be different for each drive when multiple Orchestrator instances are configured with different authentication contexts.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Users/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetCurrentUser
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Current user view permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Multiple drive paths can be specified to check the current user across different Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Multiple drive paths can be specified to check the current user across different Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet only works with non-confidential application connections (user-scoped authentication)
- Confidential application drives (app-scoped authentication) do not support this operation
- The current user may be different for each configured drive
- The RolesList property contains all roles assigned to the current user
- The Type property indicates the user account type (e.g., DirectoryUser)

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\ PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCurrentUser

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the current user information for the current Orchestrator drive.
Gets the current user information for multiple Orchestrator drives to compare authentication contexts.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $user = Get-OrchCurrentUser PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Current user: $($user.FullName) ($($user.EmailAddress))" PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Roles: $($user.RolesList -join ', ')"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the current user and displays formatted information about the user and their roles.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchCurrentUser | Select-Object UserName, FullName, EmailAddress, Type, RolesList

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the current user and displays only the key properties.
Gets the current user information and displays it in detailed list format.

maml:description.maml:para: Get the machines assigned to folders.

Outputs information about machines with specified names that are assigned to the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no machine names are specified, it outputs all machines assigned to the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Folders/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachinesForFolder(key={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Folders or OR.Folders.Read
Required permissions: (Units.View or SubFolders.View - Gets machines for any folder or only if user has SubFolders.View permission on folder)
Specifies the Name of the folder machines to be retrieved.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.
Specifies the target folder. If not specified, the current folder will be targeted.

You can specify multiple machine names, separated by commas, including wildcards. Machine names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

Exports the output to a CSV file. The CSV file will be located at the current location of the C: drive. You can customize the CSV format by combining with `select` to specify which columns to include. Try `ii c:' to open the current location of the C: drive.
Converts the output to JSON format, providing a raw view of the data from Orchestrator.
