maml:description.maml:para: Gets folder usage statistics showing entity counts in UiPath Orchestrator folders.

The Get-OrchFolderUsage cmdlet retrieves comprehensive usage statistics for UiPath Orchestrator folders, showing counts of various entities such as processes, robots, queues, assets, and other resources contained within each folder.

This cmdlet provides valuable insights for resource management, capacity planning, and organizational analysis by displaying how automation resources are distributed across the folder hierarchy. It can help identify heavily utilized folders, unused resources, and optimize resource allocation.

The cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive traversal to analyze entire folder hierarchies. It returns detailed statistics including entity types, counts, and folder paths, making it useful for auditing and reporting purposes.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - 具体的なAPIエンドポイント]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - 必要なOAuthスコープ]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - 必要な権限]

Specifies the folder paths where to search for usage information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Recursively gets usage statistics from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for usage information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Recursively gets usage statistics from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
The usage statistics help identify resource distribution patterns and can be valuable for: - Capacity planning and resource optimization
- Identifying unused or underutilized folders
- Monitoring resource growth and usage trends
- Organizational analysis and reporting
For performance analysis and reporting, consider exporting results to CSV format or using Select-Object to focus on specific metrics.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets usage statistics for the current folder.
Gets usage statistics for all folders recursively starting from the current location.
Gets usage statistics for all folders under the Production folder recursively.
Gets usage statistics for folders up to 2 levels deep from the current location.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderUsage -Recurse | Select-Object Path, Type, Count | Sort-Object Count -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets usage statistics and displays them sorted by count in descending order.
Retrieves usage statistics and displays the complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchFolderUsage -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.Type -eq "QueueItem" -and $_.Count -gt 1000} | Select-Object Path, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Finds folders with high queue item counts (over 1000) for capacity planning and queue management.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets jobs.
In the Filter parameter set, you can specify conditions for retrieving jobs using various parameters.

In the JobId parameter set, the `-Id` parameter allows you to directly specify the ID of the job to retrieve. The completion list for this `-Id` parameter only includes job IDs that have been previously retrieved and cached in memory by UiPathOrch.

After retrieving multiple jobs using the first parameter set, if you want to examine a specific job in detail, you can specify its ID using the `-Id` parameter. Even if a job entity with the specified ID exists in the cache, `Get-OrchJob` will check and display the latest state of that job.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Jobs?{filter}&$expand=Robot,Machine,Release&$orderby=CreationTime%20desc, GET /odata/Jobs({jobId})?$expand=Robot,Machine,Release

Required permissions: Jobs.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the 10 most recent jobs across all folders.

Retrieves cached jobs across all folders. Since this does not call the Orchestrator Web API, previously retrieved jobs can be displayed quickly. To fetch the latest information, specify any parameter.
Displays jobs from the past day in the current folder. The `-Last` parameter supports values like `Hour`, `Day`, `Week`, and `Month`.
Displays jobs in the current folder that are in the `Pending` or `Suspended` state. To display only failed jobs, specify `Faulted` for the `-State` parameter.
Displays jobs in the current folder that were started by a queue trigger.

Filters jobs based on their creation time using the `-CreationTimeAfter` and `-CreationTimeBefore` parameters. Other available time-based filters include `-StartTimeAfter`, `-StartTimeBefore`, `-EndTimeAfter`, and `-EndTimeBefore`.
Displays only cached jobs that were forcefully stopped.
Groups cached jobs by process name. The property names can be completed using tab completion. `group` is an alias for `Group-Object`.
Groups cached jobs by machine name.
Groups cached jobs by machine name and job status.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 11 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob | group LocalSystemAccount,State -NoElement | Format-Table -AutoSize

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups cached jobs by the account that executed them. `Format-Table -AutoSize` is used to ensure that the output is not truncated.
Groups cached jobs by creation date.
Groups cached jobs by creation month.
Groups cached jobs by the day of the week they were created.
Groups cached jobs by the day of the week they were created and their status.
Displays only cached jobs that were created on a Sunday.
Groups cached jobs by the hour they started and their status. This allows for quick analysis of when jobs were executed and their status trends.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 18 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob | group ReleaseName -NoElement | sort Count -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays grouped job information sorted by count in descending order. `sort` is an alias for `Sort-Object`.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 19 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob -State Faulted | group ReleaseName | sort Count -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups only failed jobs by process name and displays them in descending order of occurrence. This helps identify processes that fail frequently. Note: Using the `-State` parameter queries Orchestrator, so for faster processing with cached jobs, use `| ? State -eq Faulted` instead.
Sorts cached jobs in descending order by execution duration.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 21 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\Shared> Get-OrchJob | select Id, ReleaseName, @{ Name='TotalSeconds'; Expression={ ($_.EndTime - $_.StartTime).TotalSeconds }} | sort TotalSeconds -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Sorts cached jobs in descending order by execution duration and displays the execution time.
Displays the average, longest, and shortest execution times of cached jobs. `%` is an alias for `ForEach-Object`.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets job statistics from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchJobStats` cmdlet retrieves statistical information about jobs in UiPath Orchestrator. It returns a count of jobs grouped by their execution status (Successful, Faulted, Stopped, Running, Pending, Stopping, Terminating, Suspended, Resumed).
This cmdlet provides a quick overview of job execution status across the Orchestrator environment, which is useful for monitoring and reporting purposes.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetJobsStats
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: Jobs.View
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - The statistics include all job statuses: Successful, Faulted, Stopped, Running, Pending, Stopping, Terminating, Suspended, and Resumed
- The hasPermissions property indicates whether the current user has permission to view the job count for each status
- This cmdlet provides a snapshot of current job statistics at the time of execution

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchJobStats

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets job statistics for the current Orchestrator instance, showing the count of jobs by status.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $stats = Get-OrchJobStats PS Orch1:\> $stats | Where-Object {$_.count -gt 0}

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets job statistics and filters to show only statuses that have jobs with non-zero counts.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchJobStats | Sort-Object count -Descending | Select-Object title, count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets job statistics and displays them sorted by count in descending order, showing only the title and count properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $total = (Get-OrchJobStats | Measure-Object count -Sum).Sum PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Total jobs: $total"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Calculates and displays the total number of jobs across all statuses.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets library packages from Orchestrator or external host feeds.

The Get-OrchLibrary cmdlet retrieves library packages from the Orchestrator environment. Libraries are reusable components that contain activities, workflows, and other automation resources that can be shared across multiple processes.

By default, this cmdlet returns libraries from the connected Orchestrator instance. When the -HostFeed parameter is specified, it retrieves packages from external host feeds (such as the official NuGet gallery) that can be imported into Orchestrator.
The cmdlet returns essential information about each library including its ID, version, description, publication date, and metadata such as authors, tags, and project type.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Libraries
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Libraries.View
Specifies the ID of the libraries to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple IDs are specified, all matching libraries are returned.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to query libraries from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

When specified, retrieves packages from external host feeds instead of the Orchestrator library repository. This allows searching for packages that can be imported from external sources such as the official NuGet gallery.
Specifies the ID of the libraries to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple IDs are specified, all matching libraries are returned.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to query libraries from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

When specified, retrieves packages from external host feeds instead of the Orchestrator library repository. This allows searching for packages that can be imported from external sources such as the official NuGet gallery.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it retrieves information from the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
When using the -HostFeed parameter, be aware that this retrieves packages from external sources and may take longer to complete depending on network conditions and the size of the external feed.

Use ConvertTo-Json with appropriate depth settings to explore the complete structure of library objects, as they contain nested properties like ResourceTags that may not be visible in the default display format.
For inventory management and reporting purposes, consider using Select-Object to choose specific properties and Export-Csv to create reports.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all library packages from the current Orchestrator environment.
Retrieves the specific library with the ID "UiPath.Excel.Activities".
Searches for Excel-related libraries from external host feeds using wildcard matching.
Retrieves libraries from multiple Orchestrator environments (Orch1 and Orch2).

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\\> Get-OrchLibrary | Select-Object Path, Id, Version, Description | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all libraries and displays their structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.
Exports all libraries containing "Library" in their name to a CSV file for inventory management.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets specific versions of library packages from Orchestrator or external host feeds.

The Get-OrchLibraryVersion cmdlet retrieves specific versions of library packages from the Orchestrator environment or external host feeds. Unlike Get-OrchLibrary which returns the latest version of libraries, this cmdlet allows you to query all available versions of a specific library or retrieve a particular version.

This cmdlet is particularly useful for version management, dependency analysis, and when you need to work with specific versions of libraries for compatibility purposes. It can help identify all available versions of a library, check version history, and retrieve specific versions for deployment or rollback scenarios.
When used with the -HostFeed parameter, it can query external package repositories to find available versions before importing them into Orchestrator.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Libraries/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetVersions(packageId='libraryId}')
OAuth required scopes: OR.Execution or OR.Execution.Read
Required permissions: Libraries.View

Specifies the ID of the libraries whose versions will be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When specified without -Version, returns all available versions of the matching libraries.
Specifies the version(s) of the libraries to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter to filter for specific version patterns or exact versions.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to query library versions from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

When specified, retrieves library versions from external host feeds instead of the Orchestrator library repository. This allows checking available versions of packages that can be imported from external sources such as the official NuGet gallery.

Specifies the ID of the libraries whose versions will be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When specified without -Version, returns all available versions of the matching libraries.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. Use this parameter to query library versions from multiple Orchestrator environments simultaneously.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Specifies the version(s) of the libraries to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter to filter for specific version patterns or exact versions.

When specified, retrieves library versions from external host feeds instead of the Orchestrator library repository. This allows checking available versions of packages that can be imported from external sources such as the official NuGet gallery.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on tenant entities, which means it retrieves information from the entire Orchestrator tenant rather than specific folders.
When using the -HostFeed parameter, be aware that this retrieves information from external sources and may take longer to complete depending on network conditions and the size of the external feed.
This cmdlet is particularly useful for version management and dependency analysis. Use it to: - Check all available versions before upgrading or downgrading libraries
- Identify prerelease versions for testing purposes
- Analyze version history and release patterns
- Verify specific versions are available before deployment
The returned objects include IsPrerelease property to distinguish between stable and prerelease versions.

Use ConvertTo-Json with appropriate depth settings to explore the complete structure of library version objects, as they contain detailed metadata that may not be visible in the default display format.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all available versions of the "UiPath.Excel.Activities" library.
Retrieves the specific version "2.11.4" of the "UiPath.Excel.Activities" library.
Retrieves all versions of "MyLibrary" that match the pattern "1.0.*" (all 1.0.x versions).
Retrieves all available versions of "UiPath.System.Activities" from external host feeds.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion -Path Orch1:, Orch2: MyCustomLibrary

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all versions of "MyCustomLibrary" from multiple Orchestrator environments.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion MyLibrary | Sort-Object Version -Descending | Select-Object -First 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all versions of "MyLibrary" and displays the 5 most recent versions.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLibraryVersion MyLibrary | Where-Object {$_.IsPrerelease -eq $false} | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 3

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves only stable (non-prerelease) versions of "MyLibrary" and displays the detailed structure in JSON format.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the license information from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchLicense` cmdlet retrieves detailed license information from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes license expiration dates, grace period information, allowed and used license counts for different license types (Unattended, Attended, StudioPro, etc.), subscription details, and other licensing features.
The license information is essential for understanding the current licensing status, tracking usage, and ensuring compliance with UiPath licensing terms.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicense
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - License view permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - The ExpireDate and GracePeriodEndDate are returned as Unix timestamps
- The Allowed and Used properties contain hashtables with license counts by type (Unattended, Attended, StudioPro, etc.)
- License types include: ProcessOrchestration, AppTest, Headless, Development, StudioX, NonProduction, StudioPro, TestAutomation, Unattended, AgentService, Attended, and Hosting
- The IsExpired property indicates current license validity status

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicense

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the complete license information for the current Orchestrator instance.
Retrieves the license information and displays the allowed license counts by type.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $license = Get-OrchLicense PS Orch1:\> Compare-Object $license.Allowed.GetEnumerator() $license.Used.GetEnumerator() -Property Key, Value

dev:remarks.maml:para: Compares allowed versus used licenses to identify available capacity.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $license = Get-OrchLicense PS Orch1:\> if ($license.IsExpired) { Write-Warning "License has expired!" } PS Orch1:\> $expiryDate = [DateTimeOffset]::FromUnixTimeSeconds($license.ExpireDate) PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "License expires on: $expiryDate"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Checks license expiration status and displays the expiration date.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicense | Select-Object SubscriptionPlan, SubscriptionCode, IsProOrEnterprise, UserLicensingEnabled

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets license information and displays only the subscription and licensing model details.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets named user license information from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser cmdlet retrieves named user license information from UiPath Orchestrator. Named user licenses are assigned to specific users and allow them to use UiPath robots for different robot types (Attended, StudioPro, Unattended, NonProduction, TestAutomation, etc.).
Each returned object contains information about user licensing status, machine assignments, login history, and license consumption details for different robot types.
This is a tenant-level operation that retrieves license information across the entire Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - GET /odata/LicensesNamedUser/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesNamedUser(robotType='{robotType}')]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - OR.License or OR.License.Read]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - License.View]

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve named user license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Attended", "StudioPro", "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", and others.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve named user license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Attended", "StudioPro", "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", and others.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Named user licenses are assigned to specific users for different robot types
- The -RobotType parameter supports wildcard characters for flexible filtering
- Common robot types include "Attended", "StudioPro", "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", and others
- Key properties include IsLicensed (license status), MachinesCount (assigned machines), and LastLoginDate (usage tracking)
- MachineNames array contains all assigned machines, while ActiveMachineNames contains currently active machines
- IsExternalLicensed indicates if the license is managed externally
- This cmdlet operates at the tenant level and does not require navigation to specific folders
- Use filtering operations to analyze license usage patterns and identify inactive or over-allocated users

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 1: Get all named user licenses ------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves named user license information for all robot types from the current Orchestrator tenant, grouped by robot type.

command:example.maml:title: -- Example 2: Get named user licenses for specific robot type --

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves named user license information only for Attended robot type.

command:example.maml:title: - Example 3: Get named user licenses for multiple robot types -

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves named user license information for both Attended and StudioPro robot types.

command:example.maml:title: -- Example 4: Get named user licenses with wildcard filtering --

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves named user license information for all robot types ending with "Production".

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 5: Get license details and examine structure -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first license record and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 6: Get named user licenses from specific drives ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves named user license information from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 7: Filter licensed users and show details ------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser -RobotType StudioPro | Where-Object {$_.IsLicensed -eq $true} | Select-Object UserName, MachinesCount, LastLoginDate

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets StudioPro named user licenses, filters for only licensed users, and displays key information including machine count and last login date.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 8: Check users with machine assignments -------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseNamedUser | Where-Object {$_.MachinesCount -gt 0} | Select-Object RobotType, UserName, MachinesCount, MachineNames

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all named user licenses, filters for users with assigned machines, and displays robot type, user name, machine count, and machine names.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets runtime license information for robots from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchLicenseRuntime cmdlet retrieves runtime license information for robots from UiPath Orchestrator. This cmdlet provides details about robot licensing status, including whether robots are licensed, enabled, online status, and runtime capacity for different robot types (Unattended, NonProduction, TestAutomation, etc.).
Each returned object contains comprehensive information about robot machines, including machine details, license status, runtime counts, and current execution state.
This is a tenant-level operation that retrieves license information across the entire Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - GET /odata/LicensesRuntime/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetLicensesRuntime(robotType='{robotType}')]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - OR.License or OR.License.Read]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - License.View]

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve runtime license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", "Development", and others.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter allows querying license information across multiple Orchestrator instances.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

Specifies the robot type for which to retrieve runtime license information. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common robot types include "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", "Development", and others.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Runtime licenses control the execution capacity for different robot types
- The -RobotType parameter supports wildcard characters for flexible filtering
- Common robot types include "Unattended", "NonProduction", "TestAutomation", "Development", and others
- Key properties include IsLicensed (license status), IsOnline (connectivity), Runtimes (capacity), and ExecutingCount (current usage)
- MachineScope indicates whether the machine is in Default tenant scope or PersonalWorkspace
- This cmdlet operates at the tenant level and does not require navigation to specific folders
- Use filtering and grouping operations to analyze license consumption patterns across different robot types

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information for all robot types from the current Orchestrator tenant, grouped by robot type.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 2: Get runtime licenses for specific robot type ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information only for Unattended robots.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 3: Get runtime licenses for multiple robot types ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information for both Unattended and NonProduction robot types.

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 4: Get runtime licenses with wildcard filtering ---

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information for all robot types ending with "Production".

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 5: Get license details and examine structure -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first license record and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 6: Get runtime licenses from specific drives -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves runtime license information from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

command:example.maml:title: ------ Example 7: Filter licensed robots and show details ------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseRuntime -RobotType Unattended | Where-Object {$_.IsLicensed -eq $true} | Select-Object Key, MachineName, Runtimes, ExecutingCount

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets Unattended runtime licenses, filters for only licensed robots, and displays key information including runtime capacity and current execution count.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 8: Check online status and license consumption ----
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchLicenseRuntime | Where-Object {$_.IsOnline -eq $true} | Group-Object RobotType | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all runtime licenses, filters for online robots, and groups by robot type to show license consumption by type.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the licensing usage statistics from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchLicenseStats` cmdlet retrieves historical licensing usage statistics from UiPath Orchestrator. This provides valuable insights into license consumption patterns over time, helping administrators monitor usage trends, plan capacity, and ensure license compliance.

The cmdlet returns time-series data showing license usage counts by robot type (e.g., Unattended, RpaDeveloperPro) across different time periods. This data is essential for understanding licensing patterns, identifying peak usage periods, and making informed decisions about license allocation.
The statistics include daily snapshots of license usage, allowing administrators to track how different types of licenses are consumed over time and identify trends in automation activity.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /api/Stats/GetLicenseStats?tenantId={tenantId}&days={days}
OAuth required scopes: OR.Monitoring or OR.Monitoring.Read
Required permissions: License.View
Specifies the most recent period for retrieving licensing statistics. Valid values are: 'Day', 'Week', 'Month', '3Months', '6Months', 'Year', '3Years'. If not specified, a default period is used.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the most recent period for retrieving licensing statistics. Valid values are: 'Day', 'Week', 'Month', '3Months', '6Months', 'Year', '3Years'. If not specified, a default period is used.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - License statistics provide historical usage data to help with capacity planning and compliance monitoring
- The robotType property shows license types such as "Unattended", "RpaDeveloperPro", etc., but is stored internally as numeric values
- Data represents daily snapshots of license usage, not real-time consumption
- Statistics may not include the current day depending on data processing schedules
- The count property indicates the number of licenses of that type in use on the given date
- Use ConvertTo-Json to see the complete object structure including numeric robot type values
- Valid -Last parameter values are case-sensitive: 'Day', 'Week', 'Month', '3Months', '6Months', 'Year', '3Years'
- The timestamp is provided in UTC format

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1:\ PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the default licensing usage statistics for the current Orchestrator instance.
Gets licensing usage statistics for the last week.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last "Month" | Group-Object robotType | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets monthly license statistics grouped by robot type to see usage distribution.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $stats = Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last "Month" PS Orch1:\> $stats | Where-Object robotType -eq "Unattended" | Measure-Object count -Sum

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets monthly statistics and calculates total Unattended license usage.
Gets weekly license statistics and converts to JSON format to see the complete object structure, including numeric robot type values.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 6 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $stats = Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last "3Months" PS Orch1:\> $stats | Sort-Object timestamp | Select-Object -Last 10

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets 3-month statistics and displays the 10 most recent entries.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 7 --------------------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a summary report showing total and average daily usage by robot type.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 8 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last "Year" | >> Where-Object count -gt 1 | >> Sort-Object timestamp

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets yearly statistics and filters for days with more than 1 license usage, sorted by date.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 9 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $weeklyStats = Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last "Week" PS Orch1:\> $monthlyStats = Get-OrchLicenseStats -Last "Month" PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Weekly entries: $($weeklyStats.Count), Monthly entries: $($monthlyStats.Count)"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Compares the volume of data across different time periods.
Exports 6-month license statistics to a CSV file for further analysis.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machines from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchMachine` cmdlet retrieves machine information from UiPath Orchestrator. Machines represent the compute resources where robots execute automation processes. This includes both physical machines and machine templates used for robot provisioning.

This cmdlet returns comprehensive machine information including machine types (Standard, Template), scopes (Default, PersonalWorkspace, Serverless, etc.), license slot allocations, robot user assignments, and configuration details.
The cmdlet supports filtering by machine name, expanding robot user details, and exporting results to CSV files for reporting and analysis purposes.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Machines&$expand=UpdateInfo
OAuth required scopes: OR.Machines or OR.Machines.Read
Required permissions: Machines.View
Specifies the name(s) of the machines to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all machines are returned.

Expands robot user information and displays detailed license slot allocation including Unattended Slots (UA-Slots), Non-Production Slots (NP-Slots), and Test Automation Slots (TA-Slots). This changes the display format to show more detailed machine capacity information.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export operations. Common values include UTF8, UTF8BOM, ASCII.
Exports the machine data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be used for reporting, analysis, or documentation purposes.

Expands robot user information and displays detailed license slot allocation including Unattended Slots (UA-Slots), Non-Production Slots (NP-Slots), and Test Automation Slots (TA-Slots). This changes the display format to show more detailed machine capacity information.
Specifies the name(s) of the machines to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all machines are returned.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export operations. Common values include UTF8, UTF8BOM, ASCII.
Exports the machine data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be used for reporting, analysis, or documentation purposes.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Machine types include Standard (physical machines) and Template (machine templates for robot provisioning)
- Scopes define the machine's purpose: Default, PersonalWorkspace, Serverless, AgentService, or AutomationCloud
- The -ExpandRobotUser parameter changes the display format to show license slot details
- License slots indicate capacity: UnattendedSlots (UA-Slots), NonProductionSlots (NP-Slots), TestAutomationSlots (TA-Slots)
- Personal workspace machines are automatically created for individual users
- Machine templates are used for robot autoprovisioning and serverless execution
- Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete object structure including nested properties

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machines from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchMachine` cmdlet retrieves machine information from UiPath Orchestrator. Machines represent the compute resources where robots execute automation processes. This includes both physical machines and machine templates used for robot provisioning.

This cmdlet returns comprehensive machine information including machine types (Standard, Template), scopes (Default, PersonalWorkspace, Serverless, etc.), license slot allocations, robot user assignments, and configuration details.
The cmdlet supports filtering by machine name, expanding robot user details, and exporting results to CSV files for reporting and analysis purposes.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the encoding for CSV export operations. Common values include UTF8, UTF8BOM, ASCII.
Exports the machine data to the specified CSV file path. The exported CSV can be used for reporting, analysis, or documentation purposes.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets machine runtime sessions from UiPath Orchestrator.

Retrieves machine runtime sessions from UiPath Orchestrator, showing connection status and runtime information for machines across folders. This cmdlet provides information about machine connectivity, runtime availability, and session details including robot execution capacity.
Primary Endpoint: /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimesByFolderId(folderId={folderId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read
Required permissions: (Machines.View or Jobs.Create)
This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either navigation to the target folder or specification of target folders using -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for machine sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the status of the machine sessions to be retrieved. Common values include Connected, Disconnected, and others. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching.
Specifies how progress information is displayed during the operation.
Specifies the depth for recursion into the target folders. A depth of 0 indicates the current location only, with no subfolders included.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for machine sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies that the operation should include the target folder and all its subfolders.
Specifies the status of the machine sessions to be retrieved. Common values include Connected, Disconnected, and others. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching.
Specifies how progress information is displayed during the operation.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires either: - Navigation to the target folder using Set-Location (cd), OR - Specification of target folders using the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters Important: For optimal PowerShell IntelliSense support, specify -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth before other parameters when using multiple parameters. Machine Session Information: - SessionId: Unique identifier for the machine session
- Status: Connection status (Connected, Disconnected, etc.)
- RuntimeType: Type of runtime (Headless, AutomationCloud, etc.)
- Runtimes: Total available runtime slots
- UsedRuntimes: Currently used runtime slots
- MaintenanceMode: Current maintenance mode setting Common Status Values: - Connected: Machine is actively connected
- Disconnected: Machine is not currently connected
- Busy: Machine is actively executing jobs Use Cases: - Monitor machine connectivity across environments
- Check runtime availability and utilization
- Troubleshoot machine connection issues

- Capacity planning for robot deployment Important Note about Path Selection: When using Select-Object with folder entities, always include Path as the first property to identify which folder each entity belongs to. This is essential for managing entities across multiple folders.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete machine session object structure including detailed connection information and runtime statistics.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine sessions in the current folder.
Gets machine sessions from all folders in the current drive.
Gets only connected machine sessions from all folders.
Gets disconnected machine sessions from the Shared folder.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 5 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchMachineSession -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.Runtimes -gt 0} | Select-Object Path, MachineName, Runtimes, UsedRuntimes

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all machine sessions with available runtimes and displays capacity information. Note that Path is selected first to identify which folder each entity belongs to.
Gets machine sessions and displays the complete object structure including detailed session information.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the personal workspaces.

The `Get-OrchPersonalWorkspace` cmdlet retrieves personal workspace information from UiPath Orchestrator. Personal workspaces are individual automation environments assigned to specific users, providing isolated spaces for development, testing, and personal automation activities.

The cmdlet returns information about personal workspaces including workspace identifiers, names, ownership details, activity status, and last login information. Personal workspaces enable users to have dedicated environments for automation development and testing without affecting shared organizational resources.

Personal workspaces support individual user productivity by providing isolated environments where users can develop, test, and manage their automation projects independently. The workspace information includes ownership tracking and activity monitoring to help administrators manage workspace usage and lifecycle.
The cmdlet provides insights into workspace allocation, user activity, and workspace management across the Orchestrator environment, which is essential for resource planning and user management.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/PersonalWorkspaces

Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Personal workspace view permissions]

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the queues.

The `Get-OrchQueue` cmdlet retrieves queue information from UiPath Orchestrator. Queues are data repositories that enable robots to exchange information and work items in an organized manner, supporting both attended and unattended automation scenarios.

This cmdlet returns comprehensive queue information including queue definitions, retry policies, encryption settings, SLA configurations, retention policies, and organizational unit assignments. Queues support various data schemas for structured content and provide mechanisms for item processing with retry logic and error handling.

The cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive retrieval across folder hierarchies using the -Recurse parameter. It provides detailed queue metadata essential for managing automation workflows and data processing pipelines.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/QueueDefinitions
OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Read
Required permissions: Queues.View

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the queue items.

The `Get-OrchQueueItem` cmdlet retrieves queue items from UiPath Orchestrator queues. Queue items represent individual work units that flow through automation processes, containing data, status information, processing results, and error details.

This cmdlet provides comprehensive filtering capabilities to retrieve specific queue items based on various criteria including status, priority, processing dates, assigned robots, and review status. It supports complex queries to analyze work item patterns, track processing performance, and investigate automation issues.

Queue items contain structured data (SpecificContent), processing exceptions, robot assignments, review information, and audit trails. The cmdlet requires at least one filter parameter to query the Orchestrator efficiently, otherwise it displays cached results.

The cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive retrieval across folder hierarchies. It provides essential functionality for monitoring automation workflows, analyzing queue performance, and managing work item processing.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.

OAuth required scopes: OR.Queues or OR.Queues.Read
Required permissions: Queues.View and Transactions.View

maml:description.maml:para: Gets configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchSetting` cmdlet retrieves configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes various system settings such as localization, email configuration, authentication settings, deployment settings, feature toggles, and other operational parameters.
Settings control various aspects of Orchestrator behavior including mail server configuration, timezone settings, authentication policies, trigger settings, and feature availability.

Multiple values for the -Name and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - Settings OData endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Settings read scopes]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Settings view permissions]
Specifies the name(s) of the settings to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all settings are returned.
Specifies the folder path(s) to search for settings. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths. If not specified, the current location is used.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - Settings include system-wide configuration for various Orchestrator features

- Common setting categories include: Localization (Abp.Localization. ), Email (Abp.Net.Mail. ), Authentication (Auth. ), Deployment (Deployment. ), Features (Features. ), and Triggers (Triggers. ) - Some setting values may be masked for security (shown as ••••••••••••)
- The Scope property indicates the scope level of the setting
- Settings control fundamental Orchestrator behavior and should be modified carefully

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test case definitions from the Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchTestCase cmdlet retrieves test case definitions from the Orchestrator environment. Test cases are workflow files (.xaml) that have been designated as test cases within automation packages and can be executed as part of test sets for automated testing scenarios.

This cmdlet operates on folder entities and supports recursive traversal to find test cases across different folder hierarchies. Test cases are associated with specific packages and versions, enabling version-controlled testing and quality assurance processes.

Test case definitions include metadata about the associated package, version information, creation and modification details, and feed associations. These test cases can be organized into test sets and executed to validate automation workflows.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/TestCaseDefinitions
OAuth required scopes: OR.TestSets or OR.TestSets.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - 必要な権限]

Specifies the names of the test case definitions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test case definitions will be returned.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test case definitions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Recursively gets test case definitions from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.
Specifies the maximum depth level for recursive operations. Use this parameter to limit how deep the recursive search should go when -Recurse is specified.

Specifies the names of the test case definitions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. When multiple names are specified, all matching test case definitions will be returned.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test case definitions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.
Recursively gets test case definitions from subfolders. When specified, the cmdlet traverses the entire folder hierarchy starting from the specified path or current location.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
Test case definitions are associated with automation packages and contain important version information: - PackageIdentifier: The automation package containing the test case
- AppVersion: The application version of the test case
- CreatedVersion: The package version when the test case was created
- LatestVersion: The most recent version of the associated package
- FeedId: The package feed association
Parameter placement is important for folder entity cmdlets. Place -Path, -Recurse, -Depth parameters immediately after the cmdlet name for proper auto-completion functionality.
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of test case definition objects, as they contain detailed metadata including version tracking and package associations.
Test case definitions serve as the foundation for automated testing workflows and can be organized into test sets for comprehensive quality assurance processes.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all test case definitions from the current folder.
Retrieves the test case definition named "LoginTest.xaml" from the current folder.
Retrieves all test case definitions whose names contain "Test" using wildcard matching.
Retrieves all test case definitions from the current folder and all subfolders recursively.
Retrieves all test case definitions from the specified workspace and its subfolders.
Retrieves test case definitions recursively but limits the search to 2 levels deep.
Finds all test cases associated with a specific automation package.
Retrieves all test case definitions and displays their complete structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 9 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTestCase -Recurse | Select-Object Path, Name, PackageIdentifier, AppVersion, CreationTime | Format-Table

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays test case definitions with key properties in a formatted table.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 10 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTestCase -Recurse | Group-Object PackageIdentifier | Select-Object Name, Count

dev:remarks.maml:para: Groups test cases by package identifier and shows the count for each package.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets test case execution records from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchTestCaseExecution cmdlet retrieves test case execution records from UiPath Orchestrator. Test case executions represent individual runs of automated test cases, including their execution status, timing information, input/output arguments, and related metadata.
This is a folder entity operation. You must either navigate to a specific folder using Set-Location (cd) or use the -Path, -Recurse, or -Depth parameters to specify target folders.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - Test case execution API endpoint]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - Test execution read scope]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Test case execution view permissions]
Specifies the name pattern for test case executions to retrieve. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Matches against the EntryPointPath property.
Specifies the maximum depth of recursion when used with -Recurse. Controls how many levels deep the search will go in the folder hierarchy.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test case executions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Searches for test case executions in all subfolders recursively. When specified, the cmdlet searches through the entire folder hierarchy.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.
Specifies the maximum depth of recursion when used with -Recurse. Controls how many levels deep the search will go in the folder hierarchy.
Specifies the name pattern for test case executions to retrieve. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Matches against the EntryPointPath property.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for test case executions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Searches for test case executions in all subfolders recursively. When specified, the cmdlet searches through the entire folder hierarchy.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider.

maml:alert.maml:para: - This cmdlet operates on folder entities and requires folder navigation or path specification
- For optimal performance, place -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters immediately after the cmdlet name to enable proper autocompletion
- Common execution statuses include "Passed", "Failed", "Cancelled", "Pending", and others
- InputArguments and OutputArguments contain JSON-formatted test data
- Use filtering and sorting operations to analyze test execution patterns and results
- The RuntimeType property indicates the type of robot that executed the test (e.g., "TestAutomation")
- ExecutionOrder property shows the sequence of test case execution within a test set

command:example.maml:title: --- Example 1: Get test case executions from current folder ---
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> cd Orch1:\root PS Orch1:\root> Get-OrchTestCaseExecution

dev:remarks.maml:para: Navigates to the root folder and retrieves all test case executions from that folder.

command:example.maml:title: ------- Example 2: Get test case executions recursively -------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all test case executions from all folders recursively, starting from the root folder.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 3: Get test case executions by name pattern -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves test case executions whose entry point path contains "Main" from all folders recursively.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 4: Get test case executions from specific path ----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves test case executions from the specified folder path.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 5: Get test case executions with depth limit -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves test case executions recursively but limits the search to 2 levels deep from the current folder.

command:example.maml:title: ---- Example 6: Get execution details and examine structure ----
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTestCaseExecution -Recurse | Select-Object Path, -First 1 | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first test case execution and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 7: Filter executions by status ------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTestCaseExecution -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.Status -eq "Passed"} | Select-Object Path, Id, EntryPointPath, StartTime, EndTime

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets all test case executions and filters for those with "Passed" status, displaying key execution information.

command:example.maml:title: ------------ Example 8: Get recent test executions ------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchTestCaseExecution -Recurse | Where-Object {$_.StartTime -gt (Get-Date).AddDays(-7)} | Sort-Object StartTime -Descending

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets test case executions from the last 7 days and sorts them by start time in descending order.

maml:description.maml:para: Get the triggers.

Outputs information about triggers with specified names in the target folders. The target folders can be specified using the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters. If these are not specified, the current location is used as the target folder. If no trigger names are specified, it outputs all triggers in the target folders.

Multiple values for the -Path and -Name parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
When specifying the -Path, -Recurse, and -Depth parameters, place them immediately after the cmdlet name. This placement ensures that autocomplete for subsequent parameters functions correctly.
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/ProcessSchedules, GET /odata/ProcessSchedules({processScheduleId})
OAuth required scopes: OR.Jobs or OR.Jobs.Read
Required permissions: Schedules.View

dev:remarks.maml:para: Displays all triggers in the 'Shared' folder, which is the current location.
Displays all triggers in the current folder and all its subfolders. When run in the root folder, it shows all triggers across all folders in that tenant.

Displays triggers with the names specified in the current folder and its subfolders. This is useful for identifying which folders contain triggers with a specified names. You can specify multiple trigger names, separated by commas, including wildcards. Trigger names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Displays triggers with the names specified in the specified folders. You can specify multiple folders, separated by commas, including wildcards.
Displays all triggers in Orch1: and Orch2:.
Displays output with only the selected columns. Specify multiple columns separated by commas, including wildcards. Column names can be auto-completed with [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].

Exports the output to a CSV file. The CSV file will be located at the current location of the C: drive. You can customize the CSV format by combining with `select` to specify which columns to include. Try `ii c:` to open the current location of the C: drive.
Converts the output to JSON format, providing a raw view of the data from Orchestrator.
Outputs all processes in the Orch1 tenant that use the package named Process01.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets unattended robot sessions from UiPath Orchestrator.

The Get-OrchUnattendedSession cmdlet retrieves information about unattended robot sessions from UiPath Orchestrator. Unattended sessions represent robot runtime environments that can execute processes without human intervention. Each session provides details about the robot machine, runtime type, status, and configuration.
This is a tenant-level operation that retrieves session information across the entire Orchestrator instance.
Primary Endpoint: [PLACEHOLDER - GET /odata/Sessions/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetMachineSessionRuntimes]
OAuth required scopes: [PLACEHOLDER - OR.Robots or OR.Robots.Read]
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Machines.View]

Specifies the status of the unattended sessions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common status values include "Connected", "Disconnected", "Busy", and others.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for unattended sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

Specifies the folder paths where to search for unattended sessions. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Use this parameter when you want to target specific folders without changing the current location.

Specifies the status of the unattended sessions to be retrieved. Supports wildcard characters (* and ?) for pattern matching. Common status values include "Connected", "Disconnected", "Busy", and others.
Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider during the processing of this command.

maml:alert.maml:para: This cmdlet operates on folder entities, which means you may need to navigate to specific folders or use the -Path parameter to target the desired location.
When using the -Recurse parameter with large folder hierarchies, the operation may take considerable time to complete. Consider using -Depth to limit the traversal scope if needed.
Unattended sessions represent active automation sessions running on unattended robots. This information is valuable for: - Monitoring robot utilization and performance
- Identifying bottlenecks and resource conflicts
- Capacity planning and scaling decisions
- Troubleshooting automation execution issues
Use ConvertTo-Json to explore the complete structure of session objects, as they contain detailed runtime information that may not be visible in the default display format.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves all unattended robot sessions from the current Orchestrator tenant.
Retrieves only the unattended sessions that are currently connected.
Retrieves all unattended sessions that are currently disconnected.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 4: Get sessions with multiple status values -----

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves unattended sessions that are either connected or busy.

command:example.maml:title: ----- Example 5: Get session details and examine structure -----
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchUnattendedSession | Select-Object Path, -First 1 | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 5

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the first session and displays its complete object structure in JSON format for detailed analysis.

command:example.maml:title: --------- Example 6: Get sessions from specific drives ---------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves unattended sessions from multiple specified Orchestrator drives.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 7: Filter sessions by runtime type ----------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Get-OrchUnattendedSession | Where-Object {$_.RuntimeType -eq "Unattended"} | Select-Object MachineName, Status, Runtimes

dev:remarks.maml:para: Filters sessions to show only those with RuntimeType "Unattended" and displays selected properties.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets the update settings from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchUpdateSetting` cmdlet retrieves the update configuration settings from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes the update server source, update server URL, and polling interval settings that control how Orchestrator checks for and manages updates.
These settings determine how the Orchestrator instance connects to update servers and how frequently it polls for available updates.
Multiple values for the -Path parameter can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Settings/UiPath.Server.Configuration.OData.GetUpdateSettings
OAuth required scopes: OR.Settings or OR.Settings.Read
Required permissions: [PLACEHOLDER - Update settings view permissions]
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input and supports wildcards for specifying multiple paths.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.

maml:alert.maml:para: - The UpdateServerSource indicates the type of update source (e.g., "Orchestrator")
- The UpdateServerUrl contains the full URL of the update server
- The PollingInterval specifies how often (in minutes) the system checks for updates
- These settings control the automatic update checking behavior of the Orchestrator instance

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 1 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS C:\> Set-Location Orch1: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchUpdateSetting

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the update settings for the current Orchestrator instance.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 2 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> $updateSetting = Get-OrchUpdateSetting PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Update server: $($updateSetting.UpdateServerUrl)" PS Orch1:\> Write-Host "Polling interval: $($updateSetting.PollingInterval) minutes"

dev:remarks.maml:para: Retrieves the update settings and displays the update server URL and polling interval.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 3 --------------------------
command:example.dev:code: PS Orch1:\> Get-OrchUpdateSetting | Select-Object UpdateServerSource, UpdateServerUrl, PollingInterval

dev:remarks.maml:para: Gets the update settings and displays only the key configuration properties.

command:example.maml:title: -------------------------- Example 4 --------------------------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Checks the update server source and displays appropriate information.

maml:description.maml:para: Gets users from UiPath Orchestrator.

The `Get-OrchUser` cmdlet retrieves user information from UiPath Orchestrator. This includes individual users, groups, and robot accounts that can authenticate and interact with the Orchestrator environment.

By default, this cmdlet uses the list API endpoint which provides basic user information efficiently. When the -ExpandDetails parameter is specified, the cmdlet calls individual user detail APIs to retrieve comprehensive information including detailed notification preferences, session permissions, and robot provisioning settings.

For performance reasons, detailed information is not retrieved by default. However, once retrieved with -ExpandDetails, the detailed information is cached and will be available in subsequent calls to the same cmdlet even without the -ExpandDetails parameter.
The cmdlet supports filtering by username, full name, and user type, and can export results to CSV files for reporting and analysis purposes.

Multiple values for the -UserName, -FullName, and -Path parameters can be specified using comma-separated text that includes wildcards. Additionally, you can use autocomplete for these values by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Primary Endpoint: GET /odata/Users?$expand=OrganizationUnits,UserRoles
OAuth required scopes: OR.Users or OR.Users.Read
Required permissions: Users.View
Specifies the username(s) of the users to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab]. If not specified, all users are returned.
Specifies the full name(s) of the users to retrieve. Supports wildcards and multiple values. You can use autocomplete by pressing [Ctrl+Space] or [Tab].
Specifies the name of the target drives. If not specified, the current drive will be targeted. This parameter accepts pipeline input for specifying multiple Orchestrator instances.

Specifies how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. Valid values are: SilentlyContinue, Stop, Continue, Inquire, Ignore, Suspend.
